L. 113–93, title II, § 216(c)(2)128 Stat. 1061Apr. 1, 2014“The Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services shall—“(A)publicly release an annual analysis of the top 25 laboratory tests by expenditures under title XVIII of the Social Security Act [ et seq.]; and42 U.S.C. 1395“(B)conduct analyses the Inspector General determines appropriate with respect to the implementation and effect of the new payment system for laboratory tests under section 1834A of the Social Security Act [], as added by subsection (a).”42 U.S.C. 1395m–1

children.heading: Reporting of private sector payment rates for establishment of medicare payment rates
children.label: a Reporting of private sector payment rates for establishment of medicare payment rates

children.heading: General reporting requirements
children.label: A General reporting requirements
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), beginning , and every 3 years thereafter (or, annually, in the case of reporting with respect to an advanced diagnostic laboratory test, as defined in subsection (d)(5)), an applicable laboratory (as defined in paragraph (2)) shall report to the Secretary, at a time specified by the Secretary (referred to in this subsection as the “reporting period”), applicable information (as defined in paragraph (3)) for a data collection period (as defined in paragraph (4)) for each clinical diagnostic laboratory test that the laboratory furnishes during such period for which payment is made under this part.January 1, 2016

children.label: i
children.text: no reporting is required during the period beginning , and ending ;January 1, 2020April 30, 2026

children.label: ii
children.text: reporting is required during the period beginning , and ending ; andMay 1, 2026July 31, 2026

children.label: iii
children.text: reporting is required every three years after the period described in clause (ii).

children.heading: Definition of applicable laboratory
children.label: 2 Definition of applicable laboratory
children.text: In this section, the term “applicable laboratory” means a laboratory that, with respect to its revenues under this subchapter, a majority of such revenues are from this section, section 1395(h) of this title, or . The Secretary may establish a low volume or low expenditure threshold for excluding a laboratory from the definition of applicable laboratory under this paragraph, as the Secretary determines appropriate.lsection 1395w–4 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: The payment rate (as determined in accordance with paragraph (5)) that was paid by each private payor for the test during the period.

children.label: ii
children.text: The volume of such tests for each such payor for the period.

children.heading: Exception for certain contractual arrangements
children.label: B Exception for certain contractual arrangements
children.text: Such term shall not include information with respect to a laboratory test for which payment is made on a capitated basis or other similar payment basis during the data collection period.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), in this section, the term “data collection period” means a period of time, such as a previous 12 month period, specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: In the case of the reporting period described in paragraph (1)(B)(ii) with respect to clinical diagnostic laboratory tests that are not advanced diagnostic laboratory tests, the term “data collection period” means the period beginning , and ending .January 1, 2025June 30, 2025

children.heading: Treatment of discounts
children.label: 5 Treatment of discounts
children.text: The payment rate reported by a laboratory under this subsection shall reflect all discounts, rebates, coupons, and other price concessions, including those described in .section 1395w–3a(c)(3) of this title

children.heading: Ensuring complete reporting
children.label: 6 Ensuring complete reporting
children.text: In the case where an applicable laboratory has more than one payment rate for the same payor for the same test or more than one payment rate for different payors for the same test, the applicable laboratory shall report each such payment rate and the volume for the test at each such rate under this subsection. Beginning with , the Secretary may establish rules to aggregate reporting with respect to the situations described in the preceding sentence.January 1, 2019

children.heading: Certification
children.label: 7 Certification
children.text: An officer of the laboratory shall certify the accuracy and completeness of the information reported under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: A health insurance issuer and a group health plan (as such terms are defined in ).section 300gg–91 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: A Medicare Advantage plan under part C.

children.label: C
children.text: A medicaid managed care organization (as defined in ).section 1396b(m) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that an applicable laboratory has failed to report or made a misrepresentation or omission in reporting information under this subsection with respect to a clinical diagnostic laboratory test, the Secretary may apply a civil money penalty in an amount of up to $10,000 per day for each failure to report or each such misrepresentation or omission.

children.heading: Application
children.label: B Application
children.text: The provisions of (other than subsections (a) and (b)) shall apply to a civil money penalty under this paragraph in the same manner as they apply to a civil money penalty or proceeding under .section 1320a–7a of this titlesection 1320a–7a(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: as the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out this section;

children.label: B
children.text: to permit the Comptroller General to review the information provided;

children.label: C
children.text: to permit the Director of the Congressional Budget Office to review the information provided; and

children.label: D
children.text: to permit the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission to review the information provided.

children.heading: Protection from public disclosure
children.label: 11 Protection from public disclosure
children.text: A payor shall not be identified on information reported under this subsection. The name of an applicable laboratory under this subsection shall be exempt from disclosure under .section 552(b)(3) of title 5

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 12 Regulations
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall establish through notice and comment rulemaking parameters for data collection under this subsection.June 30, 2015

children.heading: Payment for clinical diagnostic laboratory tests
children.label: b Payment for clinical diagnostic laboratory tests

children.heading: Use of private payor rate information to determine medicare payment rates
children.label: 1 Use of private payor rate information to determine medicare payment rates

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (3) and subsections (c) and (d), in the case of a clinical diagnostic laboratory test furnished on or after , the payment amount under this section shall be equal to the weighted median determined for the test under paragraph (2) for the most recent data collection period.January 1, 2017

children.heading: Application of payment amounts to hospital laboratories
children.label: B Application of payment amounts to hospital laboratories
children.text: The payment amounts established under this section shall apply to a clinical diagnostic laboratory test furnished by a hospital laboratory if such test is paid for separately, and not as part of a bundled payment under section 1395(t) of this title.l

children.heading: Calculation of weighted median
children.label: 2 Calculation of weighted median
children.text: For each laboratory test with respect to which information is reported under subsection (a) for a data collection period, the Secretary shall calculate a weighted median for the test for the period, by arraying the distribution of all payment rates reported for the period for each test weighted by volume for each payor and each laboratory.

children.heading: Phase-in of reductions from private payor rate implementation
children.label: 3 Phase-in of reductions from private payor rate implementation

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Payment amounts determined under this subsection for a clinical diagnostic laboratory test for each of 2017 through 2029 shall not result in a reduction in payments for a clinical diagnostic laboratory test for the year of greater than the applicable percent (as defined in subparagraph (B)) of the amount of payment for the test for the preceding year.

children.label: i
children.text: for each of 2017 through 2020, 10 percent;

children.label: ii
children.text: for each of 2021 through 2026, 0 percent; and

children.label: iii
children.text: for each of 2027 through 2029, 15 percent.

children.heading: No application to new tests
children.label: C No application to new tests
i
children.text: A new test under subsection (c).

children.label: ii
children.text: A new advanced diagnostic test (as defined in subsection (d)(5)) under subsection (d).1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “laboratory”.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (3), once established for a year following a data collection period, the payment amounts under this subsection shall continue to apply until the year following the next data collection period.

children.heading: Other adjustments not applicable
children.label: B Other adjustments not applicable
children.text: The payment amounts under this section shall not be subject to any adjustment (including any geographic adjustment, budget neutrality adjustment, annual update, or other adjustment).

children.heading: Sample collection fee
children.label: 5 Sample collection fee
children.text: In the case of a sample collected from an individual in a skilled nursing facility or by a laboratory on behalf of a home health agency, the nominal fee that would otherwise apply under section 1395(h)(3)(A) of this title shall be increased by $2.l

children.heading: Payment for new tests that are not advanced diagnostic laboratory tests
children.label: c Payment for new tests that are not advanced diagnostic laboratory tests
A
children.text: using cross-walking (as described in section 414.508(a) of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation) to the most appropriate existing test under the fee schedule under this section during that period; or

children.label: B
children.text: if no existing test is comparable to the new test, according to the gapfilling process described in paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: Charges for the test and routine discounts to charges.

children.label: B
children.text: Resources required to perform the test.

children.label: C
children.text: Payment amounts determined by other payors.

children.label: D
children.text: Charges, payment amounts, and resources required for other tests that may be comparable or otherwise relevant.

children.label: E
children.text: Other criteria the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Additional consideration
children.label: 3 Additional consideration
children.text: In determining the payment amount under crosswalking or gapfilling processes under this subsection, the Secretary shall consider recommendations from the panel established under subsection (f)(1).

children.heading: Explanation of payment rates
children.label: 4 Explanation of payment rates
children.text: In the case of a clinical diagnostic laboratory test for which payment is made under this subsection, the Secretary shall make available to the public an explanation of the payment rate for the test, including an explanation of how the criteria described in paragraph (2) and paragraph (3) are applied.

children.heading: Payment for new advanced diagnostic laboratory tests
children.label: d Payment for new advanced diagnostic laboratory tests

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of an advanced diagnostic laboratory test for which payment has not been made under the fee schedule under section 1395(h) of this title prior to , during an initial period of three quarters, the payment amount for the test for such period shall be based on the actual list charge for the laboratory test.lApril 1, 2014

children.heading: Actual list charge
children.label: B Actual list charge
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “actual list charge”, with respect to a laboratory test furnished during such period, means the publicly available rate on the first day at which the test is available for purchase by a private payor.

children.heading: Special rule for timing of initial reporting
children.label: 2 Special rule for timing of initial reporting
children.text: With respect to an advanced diagnostic laboratory test described in paragraph (1)(A), an applicable laboratory shall initially be required to report under subsection (a) not later than the last day of the second quarter of the initial period under such paragraph.

children.heading: Application of market rates after initial period
children.label: 3 Application of market rates after initial period
children.text: Subject to paragraph (4), data reported under paragraph (2) shall be used to establish the payment amount for an advanced diagnostic laboratory test after the initial period under paragraph (1)(A) using the methodology described in subsection (b). Such payment amount shall continue to apply until the year following the next data collection period.

children.heading: Recoupment if actual list charge exceeds market rate
children.label: 4 Recoupment if actual list charge exceeds market rate
children.text: With respect to the initial period described in paragraph (1)(A), if, after such period, the Secretary determines that the payment amount for an advanced diagnostic laboratory test under paragraph (1)(A) that was applicable during the period was greater than 130 percent of the payment amount for the test established using the methodology described in subsection (b) that is applicable after such period, the Secretary shall recoup the difference between such payment amounts for tests furnished during such period.

children.label: A
children.text: The test is an analysis of multiple biomarkers of DNA, RNA, or proteins combined with a unique algorithm to yield a single patient-specific result.

children.label: B
children.text: The test is cleared or approved by the Food and Drug Administration.

children.label: C
children.text: The test meets other similar criteria established by the Secretary.

children.heading: Temporary codes for certain new tests
children.label: 1 Temporary codes for certain new tests

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall adopt temporary HCPCS codes to identify new advanced diagnostic laboratory tests (as defined in subsection (d)(5)) and new laboratory tests that are cleared or approved by the Food and Drug Administration.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), the temporary code shall be effective until a permanent HCPCS code is established (but not to exceed 2 years).

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: The Secretary may extend the temporary code or establish a permanent HCPCS code, as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: assign a unique HCPCS code for the test; and

children.label: B
children.text: publicly report the payment rate for the test.

children.heading: Establishment of unique identifier for certain tests
children.label: 3 Establishment of unique identifier for certain tests
children.text: For purposes of tracking and monitoring, if a laboratory or a manufacturer requests a unique identifier for an advanced diagnostic laboratory test (as so defined) or a laboratory test that is cleared or approved by the Food and Drug Administration, the Secretary shall utilize a means to uniquely track such test through a mechanism such as a HCPCS code or modifier.

children.heading: Input from clinicians and technical experts
children.label: f Input from clinicians and technical experts
i
children.text: the establishment of payment rates under this section for new clinical diagnostic laboratory tests, including whether to use crosswalking or gapfilling processes to determine payment for a specific new test; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the factors used in determining coverage and payment processes for new clinical diagnostic laboratory tests; and

children.label: B
children.text: recommendations to the Secretary under this section.

children.heading: Compliance with chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: 2 Compliance with chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: The panel shall be subject to chapter 10 of title 5.

children.heading: Continuation of annual meeting
children.label: 3 Continuation of annual meeting
children.text: The Secretary shall continue to convene the annual meeting described in section 1395(h)(8)(B)(iii) of this title after the implementation of this section for purposes of receiving comments and recommendations (and data on which the recommendations are based) as described in such section on the establishment of payment amounts under this section.l

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A medicare administrative contractor shall only issue a coverage policy with respect to a clinical diagnostic laboratory test in accordance with the process for making a local coverage determination (as defined in ), including the appeals and review process for local coverage determinations under part 426 of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations).section 1395ff(f)(2)(B) of this title

children.heading: No effect on national coverage determination process
children.label: B No effect on national coverage determination process
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to the national coverage determination process (as defined in ).section 1395ff(f)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: C Effective date
children.text: This paragraph shall apply to coverage policies issued on or after .January 1, 2015

children.heading: Designation of one or more medicare administrative contractors for clinical diagnostic laboratory tests
children.label: 2 Designation of one or more medicare administrative contractors for clinical diagnostic laboratory tests
children.text: The Secretary may designate one or more (not to exceed 4) medicare administrative contractors to either establish coverage policies or establish coverage policies and process claims for payment for clinical diagnostic laboratory tests, as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 1 Implementation
children.text: There shall be no administrative or judicial review under , section 1395 of this title, or otherwise, of the establishment of payment amounts under this section.section 1395ff of this titleoo

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 2 Administration
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to information collected under this section.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 3 Funding
children.text: For purposes of implementing this section, the Secretary shall provide for the transfer, from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under , to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Program Management Account, for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2018, $4,000,000, and for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, $3,000,000. Amounts transferred under the preceding sentence shall remain available until expended.section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Transitional rule
children.label: i Transitional rule
children.text: During the period beginning on , and ending on , with respect to advanced diagnostic laboratory tests under this part, the Secretary shall use the methodologies for pricing, coding, and coverage in effect on the day before , which may include cross-walking or gapfilling methods.April 1, 2014December 31, 2016April 1, 2014
